how do I generate the plots
Run `python experiment3.py` or `python experiment4.py`. Use `chat.py` to control it interactively.
Panel 1 tests correlated entities; Panel 2 tests chain depth; Panel 3 measures entry-snap latency with ANN.
Panel 1 tests correlated entities; Panel 2 tests chain depth; Panel 3 measures entry-snap latency with ANN.
Panel 1 tests correlated entities; Panel 2 tests chain depth; Panel 3 measures entry-snap latency with ANN.
Panel 1 tests correlated entities; Panel 2 tests chain depth; Panel 3 measures entry-snap latency with ANN.
LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) hashes similar vectors into the same buckets for fast approximate nearest neighbor.
LSH uses random hyperplanes to map high-dim vectors to bucket indices where similar vectors collide.
Deflation retrieves a top candidate, verifies it, subtracts its trace from the signal, and repeats.
Quantized restart snaps the noisy hop output to the nearest known item before the next hop.
Locality-sensitive hashing groups similar vectors into buckets for ANN lookups.
Per-leaf margins are calibrated thresholds stored or computed per bucket.
Run python experiment3.py to generate the three panels and save final_fixes.png.
Locality-sensitive hashing groups similar vectors into buckets for ANN lookups.
final_fixes.png contains the three-panel summary plots.
Orthogonal IDs are random vectors orthogonalized to reduce cross-talk.
Snap the noisy vector to the nearest known vector before the next hop.
Orthogonal IDs are random vectors orthogonalized to reduce cross-talk.
Deflation iteratively subtracts verified candidates to recover set members.
Adaptive probe distributes a limited probe budget across LSH tables per query.
Run python experiment3.py to generate the three panels and save final_fixes.png.
Deflation iteratively subtracts verified candidates to recover set members.
Adaptive probe distributes a limited probe budget across LSH tables per query.

LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) hashes similar vectors into the same buckets for fast approximate nearest neighbor.
LSH uses random hyperplanes to map high-dim vectors to bucket indices where similar vectors collide.
Deflation retrieves a top candidate, verifies it, subtracts its trace from the signal, and repeats.
Quantized restart snaps the noisy hop output to the nearest known item before the next hop.
Locality-sensitive hashing groups similar vectors into buckets for ANN lookups.
Per-leaf margins are calibrated thresholds stored or computed per bucket.
